comments.text:
"We provided ICICI Lombard with complete information and all documents – police report, official FIR copy, district administration report, and media coverage. Despite this, the company is saying that the claim cannot be processed until the vehicle reaches the workshop. Our vehicle fell into a nearly 300-meter deep gorge, making it impossible to move it to a workshop ourselves. It is clear that the company is avoiding its responsibilities and trying to block the claim, even though all valid evidence is with them. We expect ICICI Lombard to take immediate and proper action in the interest of the customer.

If ICICI Lombard continues to behave this way and block claims, I will remain against their policies for life, and spreading awareness about this will be my lifelong mission. Uttarakhand has approximately 16,826 villages, and spreading the message that people should not buy policies from ICICI Lombard is not a big deal – it is now very easy to do. Today, social groups and networks in villages are very strong, so this message will reach everywhere. This clearly shows their lack of customer service and responsibility."
comments.author: @himalayanyogaayurveda6650

comments.text:
This review details my experience with unexpected charges on my ICICI Lombard car insurance policy. While the initial premium for a 200,000 INR IDV (Insured Declared Value) seemed reasonable at 9211 INR, I was surprised and disappointed to learn of an additional 3800 INR charge for a simple RC (Registration Certificate) transfer.

Here's a breakdown of my concerns:

Lack of Transparency: There was no mention of this additional fee during the policy purchase process. This unexpected cost makes budgeting for car insurance difficult.
Questionable Value: The purpose and justification for a 3800 INR charge for an RC transfer seem unclear. It feels excessive for a seemingly administrative task.
This experience has unfortunately tarnished my initial impression of ICICI Lombard. While the initial premium was competitive, these additional charges raise concerns about transparency and value for money.
comments.author: @Taken991
